jokes.text: (giggles) / (laughs) / What's so funny? / Eh, nothing. / Wait a minute.
jokes.setup: Bender's suspicious giggling when Fry is about to brush teeth
jokes.punchline: The obvious setup for what Bender has done to the toothbrush
jokes.explanation: Solid setup that plays into Bender's mischievous character. The giggling creates anticipation for the revelation.

jokes.text: Have you been using my toothbrush to polish your ass again?
jokes.setup: Fry notices metal flakes on his toothbrush and connects it to Bender's behavior
jokes.punchline: The casual 'again' implies this is a recurring problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Futurama absurdism - treats robot butt-polishing as mundane problem. 'Again' suggests recurring issue, great character consistency.

jokes.text: I would never do something like that to a friend... Every single night while he's sleeping.
jokes.setup: Bender indignantly denies the accusation with seeming sincerity
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits to doing it nightly in a quiet confession
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender sociopathy - perfect misdirection structure. The quiet confession contrasts beautifully with his loud denial.

jokes.text: Who sent you?! Why are you dressed like fry? 'cause I'm fry. Oh! Fry, you old paranoid kook.
jokes.setup: Paranoid customer questions clerk about identity
jokes.punchline: Simple logical response deflates paranoia, then calls customer paranoid
jokes.explanation: Good character beat for Fry's paranoia, solid structure but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Films in every direction at once and stores the video on a remote 15-inch floppy disc.
jokes.setup: High-tech holographic camera description
jokes.punchline: Stores data on comically outdated 15-inch floppy disc
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama tech anachronism - advanced holography with primitive storage. Good world-building humor.

jokes.text: Who said that?!
jokes.setup: Clerk is wrapping package and making small talk
jokes.punchline: Sudden paranoid outburst suggests everyone's getting infected by Fry's paranoia
jokes.explanation: Brief but effective escalation of paranoia theme. Simple but works for the moment.

jokes.text: You ate my lunch again, you tainted oyster!
jokes.setup: Standard workplace lunch theft accusation
jokes.punchline: 'Tainted oyster' as creative insult for Zoidberg
jokes.explanation: Good Hermes-specific insult that plays on Zoidberg's crustacean nature. Creative but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: You ate my lunch again, you tainted oyster!
jokes.setup: Workplace lunch theft accusation
jokes.punchline: The creative insult 'tainted oyster'
jokes.explanation: Hermes' bureaucratic fury expressed through colorful insults

jokes.text: I didn't bend your golf club. And the next time I don't bend it, it'll be over your head.
jokes.setup: Bender denies bending Amy's golf club
jokes.punchline: Threatens to 'not bend' it over her head, playing on his name/nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay on Bender's nature - he 'doesn't bend' things as a threat. Perfect character integration with clever structure.

jokes.text: All right, people... And other things
jokes.setup: Professor addresses the group formally
jokes.punchline: Catches himself to include non-human crew members
jokes.explanation: Standard inclusive language humor. Functional but not particularly inspired.

jokes.text: We gather here every day.
jokes.setup: Professor dramatically announces gathering everyone
jokes.punchline: Amy points out they meet daily anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of false drama. Simple but effective reality check.

jokes.text: He's just jealous that my ass is shinier than his teeth.
jokes.setup: Bender defends himself against toothbrush accusations
jokes.punchline: Admits to having a shiny ass while comparing it to Fry's teeth
jokes.explanation: Great callback that adds new absurd logic - comparing ass shine to teeth. Pure Bender vanity.

jokes.text: Let's go, doc, chop him up. I got a rich customer needs that kidney, stat.
jokes.setup: Hidden camera reveals mysterious activity in doorway
jokes.punchline: Casual organ harvesting operation being conducted
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation - what seemed like minor spy cam setup reveals serious crime. Good misdirection.

jokes.text: Don't try to change the subject.
jokes.setup: Fry discovers Bender stole his kidney from spy footage
jokes.punchline: Bender treats kidney theft as less important than toothbrush issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priorities - organ theft is a distraction from the real issue (toothbrush). Brilliant character consistency.

jokes.text: That's the most uncomfortable sleeping bag I ever slept in.
jokes.setup: Amy emerges from punching bag she's been living in
jokes.punchline: Treats punching bag as merely uncomfortable sleeping bag
jokes.explanation: Good understatement - treating bizarre living situation as minor inconvenience.

jokes.text: What did the violence solve?
jokes.setup: Amy admits to bending Hermes' golf club in retaliation
jokes.punchline: Philosophical question about violence while having just committed property damage
jokes.explanation: Amy's pseudo-philosophical moment right after admitting to revenge. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: I thought it was one of hermes' exotic lunches!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg ate someone's kidney
jokes.punchline: Mistook human organ for Hermes' unusual food
jokes.explanation: Good character logic for Zoidberg - his alien perspective makes kidney seem like exotic food.

jokes.text: That's not an excuse. It's just an explanation of what happened.
jokes.setup: Amy says she gets hungry at 11:30 as reason for eating kidney
jokes.punchline: Clarifies she's not making an excuse, just explaining
jokes.explanation: Good distinction between excuse and explanation. Amy's matter-of-fact delivery works well.

jokes.text: No wonder my urine smells like a meal.
jokes.setup: Scruffy discovers Amy's kidney was transplanted into him
jokes.punchline: Explains his urine's unusual food-like smell
jokes.explanation: Gross but logical conclusion to kidney-eating subplot. Good Scruffy deadpan delivery style.

jokes.text: But first I have to see a man about some ground beef.
jokes.setup: Professor announces solution to trust problems
jokes.punchline: Euphemism for bathroom break, but 'ground beef' makes it disturbing
jokes.explanation: Bathroom euphemism with disgusting twist. Fits Professor's odd speech patterns.

jokes.text: I hope you're happy!
jokes.setup: Professor announces mandatory team-building retreat
jokes.punchline: Amy's sarcastic response to being forced into team-building
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic complaint. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Who's she? I don't know.
jokes.setup: New employee Jackie Jr. is introduced as part of the team
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know who a team member is
jokes.explanation: Simple honesty gag. Brief but effective awkwardness.

jokes.text: Mm, just don't drop me off on an asteroid like the last guy did.
jokes.setup: Random hitchhiker is offered a business retreat opportunity
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to previous traumatic hitchhiking experience
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor - hitchhiking in space has unique hazards. Sets up character's wariness.

jokes.text: I thought we installed alien-proof air vents!
jokes.setup: Alien creature escapes through the ship's ventilation
jokes.punchline: Professor's complaint about inadequate alien-proofing
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi world-building humor. Treats alien invasion as home improvement issue.

jokes.text: It's on my list.
jokes.setup: Professor complains about inadequate alien-proofing
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's casual acknowledgment that it's a pending maintenance item
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy response - treats deadly alien invasion as routine maintenance. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: That took longer than I expected.
jokes.setup: Jackie Jr. gets killed by the alien monster
jokes.punchline: Professor treats murder timing as minor scheduling issue
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - treating death as minor inconvenience. Perfect Professor callousness.

jokes.text: It really could have come in handy, like, 50 times.
jokes.setup: They discover the ship has a panic room they never knew about
jokes.punchline: Leela notes it would have been useful in their many previous disasters
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-reference to the show's history of dangerous situations. Acknowledges series continuity.

jokes.text: We'll be dead within an hour.
jokes.setup: Professor says they'll be safe with food and games for weeks
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with death prediction
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor contradiction. Good structure with immediate reversal of optimistic statement.

jokes.text: The name of the game is candy land.
jokes.setup: Dramatic survival situation with team assignments
jokes.punchline: Professor starts playing children's board game instead
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectations. Cuts from life-or-death mission to childish board game.

jokes.text: I don't want you locking me out there like hal.
jokes.setup: Bender wants Fry to go first into space
jokes.punchline: Reference to HAL 9000 from 2001: A Space Odyssey
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference. Works but isn't particularly clever or twisted.

jokes.text: Well, you can take your lack of trust and jam it in your kidney hole.
jokes.setup: Fry doesn't trust Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender references Fry's missing kidney in his insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback insult. Bender weaponizes his own crime in the comeback. Creative and character-specific.

jokes.text: Who needs teamwork when you got bender?
jokes.setup: Bender successfully completes task alone
jokes.punchline: Boasts about not needing teamwork right before needing help
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for Bender's inevitable comeuppance. Good ironic timing before his failure.

jokes.text: If the creature kills me, I just hope it doesn't make me into a sandwich and leave it in the fridge where some crawfish can get at it.
jokes.setup: Hermes fears death by alien creature
jokes.punchline: Specific fear about becoming sandwich that Zoidberg might steal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of lunch theft complaint into existential fear. Perfect Hermes priorities and callback integration.

jokes.text: Jackie jr.'s skull is blocking the doodad.
jokes.setup: They need to fix the ship's systems
jokes.punchline: Treats human skull as minor mechanical obstruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect casual treatment of death. 'Doodad' makes the technical term sound more dismissive.

jokes.text: Oh, it's just a chinchilla. What a cute little... (screeches)
jokes.setup: Scary noise turns out to be harmless chinchilla
jokes.punchline: Chinchilla immediately attacks, subverting the relief
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection gag. False relief immediately subverted by attack. Well-structured timing.

jokes.text: Unless you posted it on facebook. Like the last secret I told you.
jokes.setup: Amy mentions monster won't know about basement
jokes.punchline: References Leela's history of oversharing secrets online
jokes.explanation: Modern social media humor. Somewhat predictable but fits the character dynamic.

jokes.text: Lots of people wear a jockstrap thinking it's a thong.
jokes.setup: Amy explains what secret Leela shared
jokes.punchline: Reveals embarrassing underwear confusion
jokes.explanation: Absurd but believable mistake. Good embarrassing detail for character revelation.

jokes.text: Let's call ourselves frender.
jokes.setup: Fry and Bender successfully work together
jokes.punchline: Bender suggests team name combining their names (Fry + Bender)
jokes.explanation: Standard portmanteau joke. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Yay, lamey!
jokes.setup: Leela and Amy work together successfully
jokes.punchline: Amy's team name is self-deprecatingly negative (Leela + Amy = Lamey)
jokes.explanation: Better portmanteau than Frender - self-aware and self-deprecating. Amy's enthusiasm for bad name is charming.

jokes.text: This is a job for hermberg.
jokes.setup: Hermes and Zoidberg need to work together
jokes.punchline: Hermes creates team name (Hermes + Zoidberg = Hermberg)
jokes.explanation: Completes the portmanteau trio. Least creative of the three team names.

jokes.text: I guess I'll be dizzy and uncoordinated for the rest of my... Yeah, it's fixed!
jokes.setup: Bender laments his broken gyroscope will permanently affect him
jokes.punchline: Crash landing immediately fixes the problem
jokes.explanation: Good slapstick setup and payoff. Problem solving through violence is satisfying.

jokes.text: There never was a creature. Surprise.
jokes.setup: Entire episode's alien threat
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was all fake team-building exercise
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist that recontextualizes entire episode. Good meta-commentary on team building.

jokes.text: Aw, I couldn't let anything happen to something that shiny.
jokes.setup: Bender thanks Fry for saving him
jokes.punchline: Fry references Bender's shiny ass from earlier
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that shows Fry's genuine affection. Turns running gag into moment of friendship.

jokes.text: I weren't fooled. The real professor wun't never've...
jokes.setup: Shape-shifting creature impersonates Professor
jokes.punchline: Scruffy claims he wasn't fooled but immediately gets eaten mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing gag. Scruffy's confidence immediately undermined. Good grammar error for character.

jokes.text: One of us must be the bad creature. Is it me maybe?
jokes.setup: Group realizes creature could be anyone
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg innocently wonders if he might be the monster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg innocence. His genuine confusion about his own identity is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: Is it me maybe? (growling) aw. (creature reveals itself and eats Zoidberg)
jokes.setup: Paranoid self-doubt about being the creature
jokes.punchline: Disappointed realization followed by being eaten
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg - disappointed he's not special, even as monster food

jokes.text: I'm the monster.
jokes.setup: Leela is acting suspicious and biting during kiss
jokes.punchline: Fry concludes he must be the monster instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. Fry's illogical conclusion subverts audience expectation perfectly. Great Fry logic.

jokes.text: All right, fine, you got me. I can't keep track of every little detail.
jokes.setup: Fry notices Bender has ears he shouldn't have
jokes.punchline: Bender casually admits to being bad at shapeshifting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - treats shapeshifting failure as minor inconvenience. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: And sometimes at night, I unpolish it with sandpaper.
jokes.setup: Fry admits jealousy of Bender's shiny ass
jokes.punchline: Reveals he sabotages the shininess with sandpaper
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of running gag. Creates absurd cycle: Bender polishes, Fry unpolishes. Brilliant callback payoff.

jokes.text: But he uses his shape-shifting for team-building, not evil.
jokes.setup: Explains that partner is indeed a shape-shifter
jokes.punchline: Distinguishes between evil and corporate shapeshifting
jokes.explanation: Good corporate satire - treating team building as more benign than actual evil. Smart distinction.

jokes.text: Four percent increase in productivity minimum.
jokes.setup: Describes elaborate fake alien attack team building
jokes.punchline: Modest productivity gain from traumatic experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate speak. Reduces psychological trauma to minor productivity metric. Great satire.

jokes.text: Dan mcmasters was last seen two weeks ago and authorities are now calling his disappearance a homicide.
jokes.setup: Characters discuss keeping secret about what happened
jokes.punchline: News reveals the 'fake' instructor is actually missing/dead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark twist. Recontextualizes entire episode - maybe it wasn't fake after all. Excellent final reversal.

jokes.text: Total immunity for the one who turns in the other plus a double reward of $2 million.
jokes.setup: News mentions reward for information about McMasters
jokes.punchline: Police offer deal that encourages betrayal between accomplices
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of dark ending. Sets up potential betrayal between Fry and Bender.
